title: Card
description: Represents a generic card in the Digital Mao game. The card structure is intentionally minimal - all card attributes (suits, ranks, etc.) are defined by the game's initial rules, not hardcoded in this schema. This radical design allows the card system itself to be rules-based and evolvable.

id.description: Unique identifier for the card instance

properties.description: Generic properties object containing card attributes as defined by the game's card system rules. Common properties include 'suit', 'rank', 'displayName', but the schema does not enforce these - they are defined by rules.

x-design-philosophy.radical-rules-based: This schema intentionally avoids hardcoding ANY card attributes (suits, ranks, etc.). The card is simply an object with an ID and a generic properties map. The actual card system (what suits exist, what ranks exist, how many cards in a deck) is defined entirely by the game's initial rules. This allows for ultimate flexibility - the same schema could theoretically support standard playing cards, Tarot decks, Uno cards, or completely custom card systems.
benefits: Card system can evolve through rule changes
No assumptions about card structure beyond having properties
Supports any card game, not just standard playing cards
True to Mao's philosophy of discovering rules through play
